description: Romance requires specific element configurations centered on a romantic relationship between at least two characters whose emotional journey is the story's primary engine.

character_constraints.description: At least one protagonist whose emotional journey drives the story.
A second central character who is the object/partner of the romantic connection. In dual-protagonist romance, both are protagonists.
An external force opposing the relationship — a rival, family pressure, societal norm, or circumstance.
A trusted friend each lead can confide in about their feelings.

relationship_constraints.description: The central romantic relationship must exist between the two leads.
A competing romantic interest or obstacle to the central pairing.

place_constraints.description: The leads' separate starting worlds that establish who they are before the relationship.
A shared space where the relationship develops — a workplace, event, or journey.

object_constraints.description: An object representing the state of the relationship — a ring, letter, shared memento.

element_rules.description: The romantic relationship must be the primary plot driver, not a subplot.
element_rules.testable_condition: The romantic relationship between the leads is referenced in at least 60% of timeline moments.

element_rules.description: Both leads must have independent agency and motivations beyond the romance.
element_rules.testable_condition: Each lead character has at least 2 motivations, and at least 1 is not directly about the other lead.

element_rules.description: The story must end with the leads together (HEA/HFN) or with a clear resolution of the romantic tension.
element_rules.testable_condition: The final timeline moment includes both leads as participants with a 'bonds' transition.

element_rules.description: There must be at least one significant obstacle to the relationship that creates genuine doubt.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one timeline moment contains a 'breaks' transition between the leads.
